differences: Generated HUD keeps one primary dispersed neural-cloud state instead of exposing multiple simulated modes.
Clicking the cloud changes morphology, motion speed, and electrical pulse intensity within that state.
The coalescing control pulls the original neural cloud boundary inward while keeping the interior filled.
M3-style rhythm is now represented by slow breathing, double-beat contraction, and outward pulse waves.
suggestions: Keep click feedback lightweight and avoid adding more visible mode buttons.
Use the coalescing toggle only for explicit assistant-needed moments.
Tune compact-cloud radius, heartbeat amplitude, and pulse brightness before adding additional state concepts.
visual_verdict.reasoning: The UI now stays close to the first-state neural cloud while adding the body rhythm from M3 Particle Voice: contraction, heartbeat, pulse, and breathing feel.
next_actions: No further visual edit required in this pass.
